history.content:
<thought>
The goal is visible ahead and to the right, but the cell directly in front of me is lava, so moving forward would be unsafe. The cell to my right is open floor, so I should rotate clockwise to face that safe path toward the goal.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am now facing right after the previous turn. The cell directly in front of me is a wall, so I cannot move forward. The goal is to my left and slightly ahead, and the open path is above me through safe floor cells. Turning left will face me toward that safe route.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I am facing upward again. The cell directly in front of me is lava, so moving forward is unsafe. The safe open route is to the right, which leads toward the goal. I should turn right to face that path.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am facing right again. The cell directly in front of me is a wall, so I cannot move forward. The safe path toward the goal is above me through open floor cells. Turning left will face that safe direction.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I am facing upward. The cell directly ahead is lava, so moving forward would be deadly. The open safe path is to my right, which also leads toward the goal. I should turn right.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The cell directly ahead is a wall, so I cannot move forward. The goal lies up and to the left, and the safe route starts by facing upward through the open cell above me. I should turn left.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I am again facing upward with lava directly in front of me, so moving forward is unsafe. The open safe cell is to my right, and that direction leads toward the visible goal. I should turn right.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The map matches the earlier view where I am facing upward with a wall directly ahead. Since forward is blocked, and the safe path toward the goal is through the open cell to my left (which becomes forward after turning left), the best next step is to turn left.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
I am facing upward again. The cell directly ahead is lava, so moving forward would be deadly. The safe open route toward the visible goal is to my right, so I should rotate clockwise.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am back at the view with a wall directly ahead, so I cannot move forward. The safe corridor leading toward the goal starts on my left side, while the right side is a wall. Turning left is the correct next step.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The cell directly ahead is lava, so moving forward is unsafe. The open path toward the goal is to the right, through the safe floor cell beside me. I should turn right to line up with that route.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
The wall is directly in front of me again, so moving forward is impossible. The safe route is to my left toward the open corridor that leads toward the goal, while the right side is blocked by a wall. I should turn left.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The view matches the earlier position where the cell directly ahead is lava. The safe progress toward the visible goal is through the open cell on my right, so I should turn right to face that path.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am back at the orientation where the cell ahead is a wall. The corridor to the left is safe and leads toward the goal, while the right side is blocked. Turning left is the correct next step.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_LEFT
</action>

<thought>
The cell directly ahead is still lava, so moving forward is unsafe. The open route toward the visible goal is to the right through the safe floor cell beside me, so I should turn right.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>
